notes: Multi-factor unlocking via an arbitrary monotone boolean access policy (AND/OR) over named factors: passwords + FIDO2 YubiKeys. Construction: one random DEK encrypts the vault; the DEK is secret-shared down the policy formula tree (OR replicates the node secret, AND XOR-splits it); each leaf share is wrapped with its factor KEK (password = Argon2id, yubikey = HKDF of the FIDO2 hmac-secret). Factors and policy are managed in-store while unlocked; interactive init prompts for a password and offers a YubiKey. Ships as 0.3.0 behind a yubikey cargo feature. Security: OR strength equals the weakest satisfying set, so password branches must stay strong.
title: YubiKey + multi-factor auth policy (0.3.0)

notes: New CypherVersion for policy-protected vaults. Header: version, flags, iv, pad_len. Body: the policy formula tree (AND/OR nodes + factor-leaf references), a table of leaves each holding factor params + wrapped share, then the DEK-encrypted payload + HMAC. Old V7 password-direct vaults keep opening. Pick a serialization (bincode like the existing header, or small TLV) that stays forward-extensible for new factor types.
title: Design v8 keyslot+policy file format

notes: Monotone boolean access structure. Tree: And(children), Or(children), Leaf(factor_id). distribute(secret, tree): OR copies the node secret to every child; AND XOR-splits into one random share per child (s1 xor ... xor sn = secret); leaves get their assigned share. reconstruct(tree, provided): leaf yields its share if the factor was provided; OR yields any present child; AND yields the XOR of all children iff all present; root yields the DEK iff satisfied. Exhaustively unit-test every satisfying and non-satisfying factor subset for representative policies including pass1 or (pass2 and yk1).
title: Policy secret-sharing engine

notes: Generate a random DEK (32B key + 32B HMAC key) at vault creation; the DEK encrypts the payload via the existing Cypher envelope. Wrap and unwrap each leaf share by reusing Cypher::new(KEK).encrypt/decrypt, whose HMAC check signals a satisfied factor. Wire the DEK and wrapped leaves into the new format.
title: DEK key hierarchy + leaf wrap via Cypher

notes: Password factor: per-factor random salt + Argon2id params; KEK = Argon2id(password, salt), reusing the existing derivation. Store the factor params in the leaf. Memory-hard because the input is a human password.
title: Password factor (Argon2id KEK)

notes: Parse a boolean expression over factor names into the formula tree, e.g. pass1 or (pass2 and yk-main). Grammar: factor names, and, or, parentheses; and binds tighter than or; recursive descent. Validate that all referenced factors exist. Render a tree back to a canonical expression for policy show.
title: Policy expression parser

notes: Enroll and remove named factors; set and show the policy. On any change, re-run the DEK distribution for the new tree and rewrite the leaves; the DEK is unchanged so the payload is not re-encrypted. Requires being unlocked (DEK in hand), like LUKS keyslot management. Persist factors, policy, and wrapped leaves into the new format.
title: Factor and policy management

notes: Add interactive in-store commands to InteractiveCli, run while unlocked, driving factor and policy management: enroll password NAME; enroll yubikey NAME with optional pin; factors; remove factor NAME; policy show; policy set EXPR. Update the help text.
title: In-store auth commands

notes: New-store creation flow: prompt for a password (factor pass1), then offer to enroll a YubiKey now; if yes, run FIDO2 enrollment and ask whether both factors are required or either (and/or) to set the initial policy. Default policy with a single password is just that factor.
title: Interactive new-store init flow

notes: On open, read the policy and prompt for a satisfying set of factors: password entry for password factors, touch plus optional PIN for yubikey factors, collecting opportunistically until the root is satisfied. Reconstruct the DEK and build the Cypher from it. Handle wrong-factor and unsatisfiable cases gracefully.
title: Policy-driven unlock UX

notes: Implement the yubikey factor with ctap-hid-fido2 behind a yubikey cargo feature. Enroll: make_credential with the hmac-secret extension (non-resident), an rp_id such as rcypher, store credential_id + per-factor salt + uv_required. Unlock: get_assertion with the credential and salt, user-presence true (touch), user-verification optional (PIN); KEK = HKDF of the hmac-secret output (no Argon2, the secret is high-entropy). Verify ctap-hid-fido2 supports hmac-secret. Hardware-validated by the maintainer.
title: FIDO2 YubiKey factor (hmac-secret)

notes: Warn when a policy has a satisfying set reachable by a single password factor alone (or otherwise weak), because OR security equals the weakest satisfying set. Surface at policy set time and on store open. Nice-to-have security UX.
title: Warn on weak policy branches

notes: Document the policy model (DEK plus formula-tree secret sharing), the in-store commands, FIDO2 enrollment, recovery and backup guidance (keep a strong recovery password branch and enroll a backup YubiKey as another factor), and the weakest-satisfying-set caveat. Update README, security docs, and CHANGELOG.
title: Docs for the auth/policy model

notes: Bump to 0.3.0 and follow docs/crate_release_process.md (publish rcypher then rcypher-cli; release.yml builds the four binaries). The yubikey feature is additive; confirm the default build, the lean core, and the macOS and ARM cross-checks stay green.
title: Release 0.3.0
